title: oxytropis adamsiana subsp. erecta
intro: Oxytropis adamsiana subsp. erecta, also known as Adams' locoweed, is a perennial plant species in the legume family, Fabaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain and swelling, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Oxytropis adamsiana subsp. erecta include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are typically made by boiling or steeping the plant's roots and leaves in water or a solvent.
